title: Day 1: The GenAI Landscape
content: Theory: Understanding the difference between LLMs (Text), Diffusion (Images), and Generative Video.
exercise: Exercise: List 5 tasks in your daily life that currently take 30+ minutes and could be "AI-assisted."

title: Day 2: The Master Prompt Formula
content: Theory: Context-Task-Constraint-Output. Why "Write a blog post" is a bad prompt.
exercise: Exercise: Use the formula to generate a 300-word article on any topic. Iterate 3 times until the tone is perfect.

title: Day 3: Chain of Thought Logic
content: Theory: Asking AI to "think step-by-step." This reduces "hallucinations" and improves logic.
exercise: Exercise: Prompt an AI to solve a complex business problem, forcing it to explain its reasoning at every step.

title: Day 4: AI for Deep Research
content: Theory: Using AI to summarize long documents and find patterns in data.
exercise: Exercise: Feed a long article or transcript into an AI and ask it to find 3 "Counter-intuitive" insights from the text.

title: Day 5: Persona Engineering
content: Theory: How to "prime" the AI to act as an expert (e.g., a Yale-educated editor or a Top-tier marketer).
exercise: Exercise: Build a "Custom Instruction" for an AI to act as a cynical, high-level business consultant.

title: Day 6: Fact-Checking & Veracity
content: Theory: Why AI lies and how to catch it. Using search-integrated tools to verify claims.
exercise: Exercise: Generate 5 "facts" about an obscure topic, then use manual search to verify if the AI was correct.

title: Day 7: Custom GPTs & Gems
content: Theory: Building your own specialized "Mini-AI" tools for repetitive tasks.
exercise: Exercise: Create an AI "Gem" or "GPT" whose only job is to rewrite your rough notes into professional emails.

title: Day 8: AI Image Generation
content: Theory: Prompting for visuals. Understanding lighting, camera angles, and style keywords.
exercise: Exercise: Generate 5 professional product shots for a fictional brand. Use keywords like "Depth of Field" and "Studio Lighting."

title: Day 9: Consistent Characters
content: Theory: The "Reference" technique. How to keep the same person or object across multiple images.
exercise: Exercise: Generate a character. Now, generate that same character in a completely different environment/outfit.

title: Day 10: AI for UI & Graphic Design
content: Theory: Using AI to brainstorm layouts, mood boards, and logo concepts.
exercise: Exercise: Use an image generator to create 3 "Mood Boards" for a tech startup brand.

title: Day 11: Text-to-Video Basics
content: Theory: Moving from static to motion. Principles of AI video prompting (pacing, motion, camera moves).
exercise: Exercise: Create a 5-second cinematic clip of a "futuristic city" using a text-to-video tool.

title: Day 12: AI Audio & Music
content: Theory: Generating background tracks and voiceovers that don't sound like robots.
exercise: Exercise: Generate a 30-second background track and a 10-second voiceover. Combine them in an editor.

title: Day 13: Social Media Assets
content: Theory: Using AI to create high-click-rate thumbnails and backgrounds.
exercise: Exercise: Use AI to generate a background for a YouTube thumbnail that is "High Contrast" and "Vibrant."

title: Day 14: The Multi-modal Workflow
content: Theory: Combining Text + Image + Video into a single production pipeline.
exercise: Exercise: Use AI to write a 15-second ad script, generate a matching image, and create a video clip.

title: Day 15: AI for Data Analysis
content: Theory: Feeding AI spreadsheets to find trends, averages, and outliers.
exercise: Exercise: Upload a CSV of data (or paste a table) and ask the AI to "Visualize the 3 most important trends."

title: Day 16: Coding with AI (No-Code)
content: Theory: Using AI to write HTML, CSS, or Python scripts even if you aren't a coder.
exercise: Exercise: Use AI to write the code for a simple "Calculator" or "Countdown Timer" and test it in a browser.

title: Day 17: AI Personal Knowledge Bases
content: Theory: Connecting AI to your own notes to build a "Second Brain."
exercise: Exercise: Ask an AI to summarize your last 5 "Exercise" responses from this course and find a common theme.

title: Day 18: AI for Email Automation
content: Theory: Integrating AI into tools like Zapier to handle communications.
exercise: Exercise: Write a "logic flow" for an AI that reads an email and decides if it's "Spam," "Urgent," or "General."

title: Day 19: Presentation Design
content: Theory: Using AI to outline, write, and suggest visuals for pitch decks.
exercise: Exercise: Use AI to generate an 8-slide outline for a product launch.

title: Day 20: Autonomous Agents
content: Theory: The future of AI. Software that can perform multi-step tasks without human input.
exercise: Exercise: Map out a "Task Chain" for an agent that researches a company and drafts a custom pitch.

title: Day 21: Content Scaling
content: Theory: 1 Concept -> 100 Assets. How to use AI to repurpose content across platforms.
exercise: Exercise: Take one long-form blog post and use AI to turn it into 5 Tweets, 3 LinkedIn posts, and 1 Reel script.

title: Day 22: Ethics & Copyright
content: Theory: Who owns AI art? Understanding the legal landscape of 2026.
exercise: Exercise: Research the "SynthID" or watermarking policies of your favorite AI tool.

title: Day 23: Personal Brand Strategy
content: Theory: Using AI to find your "Unique Angle" in a crowded market.
exercise: Exercise: Ask an AI to "Audit" your current brand idea and find 3 "Weaknesses" in your positioning.

title: Day 24: Productizing AI Services
content: Theory: How to sell "AI Workflows" to businesses as a high-ticket consultant.
exercise: Exercise: Define one "AI-Powered" service (e.g., "AI Content Engine") and list exactly what the client receives.

title: Day 25: System Audit
content: Theory: Finding the "bottlenecks" in your AI workflows. Where are you still doing too much manual work?
exercise: Exercise: Review your most-used prompt. Rewrite it to be 50% shorter but 2x more effective.

title: Day 26: Training vs. Prompting
content: Theory: Understanding Fine-tuning and RAG (Retrieval Augmented Generation) in simple terms.
exercise: Exercise: Research the difference between "uploading a file" to an AI vs. "training" an AI on that file.

title: Day 27: The Human-in-the-Loop
content: Theory: Why "100% AI" content fails. The 80/20 rule: AI does 80%, Human does the final 20%.
exercise: Exercise: Take an AI-generated post and spend 45 minutes "Humanizing" it with personal stories and unique opinions.

title: Day 28: Pitching AI Workflows
content: Theory: Selling the "Time Saved." How to convince a business owner to adopt AI.
exercise: Exercise: Write a pitch to a local business owner explaining how they can save 10 hours a week using AI.

title: Day 29: The AI Portfolio
content: Theory: How to show AI work without looking like a "cheater." Highlighting the process and results.
exercise: Exercise: Create a "Case Study" for your Day 21 exercise, showing the "Input" vs. "Output."

title: Day 30: Future-Proofing Your Skill
content: Theory: The 90-day learning loop. AI moves fast; how do you stay an "expert"?
exercise: Exercise: Set a "Learning Schedule" for the next 3 months to keep your AI skills sharp.
